strategic_capability_mapping.relationship: Gives unattended recovery tasks deterministic cargo check path, line, column, code, and severity anchors through the existing recovery flow so the next task can act without raw log inspection.

Persists only bounded structured diagnostic fields on failed verifier terminal evidence, task completion gates, and recovery provenance while continuing to exclude raw stdout, stderr, rendered messages, source snippets, commands, environment, and absolute paths.
Keeps Cargo diagnostics owned by the Rust verifier and completion gate rather than relying on LLM interpretation, caller-supplied paths, or VSIX policy decisions.
Materializes bounded recovery diagnostics into the existing prompt-building path so recovery tasks receive enough context to repair the failed compile check without reopening raw verifier output.

questions.question: Which strategic capabilities does this phase advance?
What new user-visible, operator-visible, or runtime capability does it add?
Why can existing functionality not substitute for this work?
What concrete product gap remains if this is not implemented?
Is this semantically distinct from the previous three phases?
Can this be done without adding a new RPC?
Can this be done without adding a new summary surface?
What runtime or development-control behavior actually changes?
How does this help headless autonomous development?
How does this move the milestone toward completion?

answers.strategic_capabilities: Advances headless_autonomous_development, persistent_structured_ledger, runtime_permission_enforcement, and context_management.
answers.new_capability: A failed controlled cargo check now carries up to five sanitized diagnostics with tool ID, check ID, kind, severity, optional code, workspace-relative path, line, column, and truncation state through the verifier result, ledger, completion gate, recovery provenance, and recovery prompt materialization.
answers.why_existing_functionality_is_insufficie: R3.1 and R3.2 exposed safe verifier status and recovery gates but not actionable compile locations. A caller or recovery task could know cargo check failed, but could not identify the file and position without raw output or source content.
answers.product_gap_if_skipped: Headless recovery remains forced to guess from a generic cargo_check failure, inspect raw logs outside the runtime contract, or ask for human intervention even when Cargo already produced a precise diagnostic location.
answers.non_duplicative: Yes. R3.1 fixed verifier execution metadata and redaction, R3.2 fixed recovery gate correctness, and M8.3 added explicit verifier retry. R3.3 adds bounded actionable diagnostic propagation for cargo_check failures.
answers.no_new_rpc: Yes. The implementation extends existing tool execution payloads, task.run verification gates, task records, and prompt materialization. It adds no RPC.
answers.no_new_summary: Yes. No report, digest, history, readiness wrapper, inspection surface, or external summary endpoint is added. The only new prompt material is generated from existing recovery provenance.
answers.runtime_behavior_change: Controlled cargo_check executes with structured Cargo JSON output, extracts bounded diagnostics inside the Rust runtime, sanitizes paths and fields before ledger insertion, and makes those diagnostics available to recovery task prompt construction.
answers.headless_autonomous_contribution: The next recovery task can choose targeted file reads or repair proposals from stable diagnostic anchors without consuming raw compiler text, commands, environment, absolute paths, or file content.
answers.milestone_progress: This closes the diagnostic-actionability blocker in R3 and leaves remaining milestone work focused on replay edge cases rather than raw-output leakage.

guard_engine_change_review.review_intent: R3.3 updates the deterministic current phase manifest and archives the R3.3 value gate. Review must confirm the manifest describes real bounded diagnostic propagation and does not weaken generic diagnostics or phase-value enforcement.

exit_criteria: verification.cargo_check runs with structured Cargo JSON output while retaining raw stdout/stderr only inside the verifier capture buffer
failed cargo_check verifier output includes at most five bounded_cargo_diagnostics entries with sanitized workspace-relative path, line, column, severity, kind, code, and truncated fields

tool execution ledger payload copies bounded diagnostics only after runtime sanitization and still excludes raw stdout, stderr, rendered diagnostics, source snippets, commands, environment, and absolute paths
TaskRunVerificationCompletionGate carries bounded diagnostics for failed cargo_check evidence through existing task.run results and terminal task payloads
VerificationRecoveryProvenance carries the same bounded diagnostics into admitted recovery tasks and the failure fingerprint remains replay-safe
ContextMaterializer includes bounded recovery diagnostics in recovery prompt materialization without raw compiler text or file content
VSIX protocol validation accepts valid bounded diagnostics on task.run gates, task records, and ledger payloads
VSIX protocol validation rejects raw nested diagnostic fields, absolute paths, parent traversal/protected paths, invalid line or column values, and arrays above the bound
No new RPC, report, digest, history, readiness wrapper, or inspection surface is added
No product implementation outside bounded cargo diagnostic propagation is mixed into this PR

test_tokens: accepts bounded cargo diagnostics and rejects unsafe diagnostic payloads

next_phase_after_r3_3: R3.4 Recovery Diagnostic Replay Edge Case Closure
